name: Lifetime
description_short: Life Time is a premium fitness and wellness company operating 190+ athletic country clubs nationwide, offering gym memberships, personal training, luxury amenities, and integrated healthy living experiences.
description_long: Life Time is a national chain of Athletic Country Clubs that has grown to operate over 190 locations across the United States, with the largest presence in Minnesota (where it was founded), Texas (37 clubs), Illinois (17 clubs), and California (10 clubs). The company positions itself as going "beyond a gym" to provide comprehensive health and wellness communities. Life Time offers tiered membership levels with access to athletic facilities, personal training programs, group fitness classes, and recovery amenities. Beyond traditional gym services, the company has expanded into co-working spaces (15 integrated locations), luxury residential apartments branded as part of their healthy lifestyle ecosystem, and digital health tools including their LT App and Active Metabolic Assessment services. The company distinguishes itself through premium amenities including hydrotherapy suites, infrared saunas, extensive racquet courts, and pickleball facilities (claiming the most courts of any operator), plus lifestyle content via Experience Life Magazine and LT Health resources. Life Time operates as a membership-based wellness provider rather than a financial services company, and the current "payday-alternatives" categorization appears to be an error. The company targets affluent consumers seeking comprehensive health and lifestyle integration rather than financial assistance or credit solutions.
services: Athletic country club memberships with tiered access levels
Personal training with metabolic assessment and app integration
Group fitness classes including Core, Tone, Reform (CTR) reformer workouts
Racquet sports facilities and pickleball courts
Hydrotherapy suites and infrared sauna recovery amenities
Athletic event programming and competitions across multiple formats
Digital health tools via LT App
Health and wellness content via Experience Life Magazine and LT Health
pros: Extensive national footprint with 190+ club locations across diverse markets
Integrated ecosystem combining fitness, co-working spaces, and luxury residential options
Advanced personal training programs including 60XT with metabolic assessment integration
Comprehensive luxury amenities including hydrotherapy suites and infrared saunas
Year-round athletic events and programs for multiple fitness levels
Digital integration through LT App and online health content (Experience Life Magazine)
Diverse class formats including new Core, Tone, Reform (CTR) reformer-based workouts
cons: Membership pricing and access varies significantly by location with no transparent national pricing displayed
Many amenities and services require separate or supplemental fees beyond base membership dues
Limited accessibility for budget-conscious consumers given premium positioning and luxury focus
Certain areas and facilities (outdoor pools, racquet courts) may have restricted access or additional costs
No indication of flexible or budget membership tiers comparable to mainstream gym chains
best_for: Affluent individuals seeking comprehensive wellness and lifestyle integration beyond basic gym access
Health-conscious professionals interested in integrated co-working and fitness communities
Athletes and fitness enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices for advanced amenities and programming
diagnosis: Life Time is a premium membership-based fitness and wellness operator, not a financial services or credit alternative company. The payday-alternatives categorization is incorrect—this company targets affluent consumers seeking high-end health club memberships and integrated lifestyle services, with pricing and access that varies significantly by location and typically requires supplemental fees for premium amenities.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
